helpful: Full provisioned petition text made the QP, posture, and vehicle problems (waiver, partial mootness, prior denials) directly readable without any live retrieval.
Statpack relist-count and per-fee-class cuts gave a usable anchor (zero-relist ~0.8% grant; paid ~5-7%) for a cell with no salience-band table.
gaps: The prompt points to a 'Segment base rate by salience band' statpack section that is absent from the committed statpack.md, so the promised band anchor was unavailable.
`fedcourts query --era modern` silently returns zero rows because corpus era labels are decade strings (e.g. '2020s'); the CLI accepting an unmatched era value with no note invites a wasted call.
No corpus facet for pro se status or family-law subject matter on SCOTUS rows, so the closest-prior query cannot get near this cell's true reference class.
notes: Clear-cut cell: every case-specific signal points the same way, so retrieval was kept minimal (2 distinct corpus queries, no MCP calls).
